summary: Iteration 2 (Auth + RBAC + ocupacion + recordatorios) tested end-to-end. Backend: 31/31 pytest cases PASS covering login/logout, JWT, RBAC on users/credits/reminders/backup, ocupacion CRUD, credit reassignment, atendioPor autogenerated, change-password. Frontend: login/logout flow works, admin sees 'Asesores' section, asesor does NOT; asesor creates client with ocupacion visible in list & detail; recordatorios KPIs visible (vencidas/hoy/manana/semana); asesor sees no filter (correct); wrong-current-password on Cambiar contraseña shows error. Two minor UI observations flagged for main agent (no blockers).

ui_bugs.issue: Could not find data-testid="new-credit-btn" — new-credit button likely uses a different testid. Recommend standardizing testids for creation buttons across sections.

Filter-by-asesor select IS rendered but not exposed with data-testid="rem-filter-asesor" as documented in the review request. Console shows testid begins with 'reminders-...'. Add/align data-testid for automation.

issues: Text 'Solo el administrador puede modificar estos datos' is shown, but the RUC/moneda/mora inputs appear visually editable to the asesor. Backend correctly rejects on save via require_admin, but the inputs should be rendered with disabled attribute for clearer UX.

action_items: Ensure Configuración form fields are rendered with `disabled` attribute when current user is asesor (currently only header text warns).
Standardize/align data-testids on: new-credit-btn (Créditos list) and rem-filter-asesor (Recordatorios). Documented testids should match what the code renders.

Minor: `option` element wrapping in Recordatorios filter emits a React hydration warning ('span cannot be a child of option'). Verify the select renders `<option>{u.name}</option>` as plain text, no wrappers, to avoid warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py seed_admin correctly updates existing admin password if env changed (idempotent). Good.
server.py JWT: uses HS256 with 12h TTL, secret from env. bcrypt hashing $2b$ format confirmed. Good.
server.py credits_filter(user) correctly restricts asesor view; get_credit + pagar_cuota also enforce 403 on cross-asesor access.
server.py backup/export version bumped to 2; test_cartera_creditos.py (iter 1) still asserts version==1 (kept passing? — verify. Old test file may fail if re-run alongside new one).
server.py backup/import does NOT accept version 2 users field — users are export-only. Documented; OK.
server.py reminders endpoint: sort key uses fechaVencimiento string — since fechaVencimiento is ISO, lexicographic sort matches chronological. OK.
App.js: consider disabling Configuración inputs when role != admin (see UI note).
server.py: no rate-limit / brute-force lockout on /auth/login. Not required for this iteration but flag for hardening.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all critical flows OK; only 2 minor testid gaps + 1 UX polish)

test_credentials: admin/admin123 (auto-seed). Test-created asesor via API: test_ana/ana12345 (cleaned up). Test-created asesor via UI: ana.torres/ana12345 (still in DB, has no credits).
seed_data_creation: Backend tests self-clean (delete client, credits, user at end). UI test left one client 'UI_Cliente Test' and one user 'ana.torres' in DB (no credits, so ana.torres could be removed by admin).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 auth/RBAC tests at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration2_auth_rbac.py — run with `pytest -n 0 backend/tests/test_iteration2_auth_rbac.py` for serial execution (module-scoped state fixture). Iter 1 test file (test_cartera_creditos.py) is OUTDATED (no auth headers; will 401 on most endpoints) — main agent should update or delete it. Backup version changed from 1 to 2. Admin credentials via ADMIN_USERNAME/ADMIN_PASSWORD env; do NOT rotate JWT_SECRET during testing (invalidates tokens).
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking bugs. Two data-testid mismatches with the review-request spec are documented under action_items.
